title: CLI: sealed broadcast send+receive + PUBLISH_HPKE_KEY on connect + sealed DM
description:
What: Wire sealed sender into cli/src/commands.rs chat loop: publish user HPKE key on connect, send sealed broadcasts when MLS active, receive and unseal SealedDeliver notifications, send/receive sealed DMs.

Algorithm:
1. Read /home/mark/WORK/nie/cli/src/commands.rs (full chat() function)
2. At chat loop startup (after auth handshake, after MLS init):
   a. Generate per-session user HPKE note: the Identity struct now has hpke_pub_key_bytes()
   b. Send PUBLISH_HPKE_KEY { public_key: identity.hpke_pub_key_bytes().to_vec() }
   c. Store identity.hpke_secret_bytes() as hpke_secret for later unseal
3. Track room HPKE key state:
   let mut room_hpke_pub: Option<[u8; 32]> = None;
4. In the send path (when user types a message and MLS is active):
   a. Encrypt via MLS: mls_ciphertext = mls_client.encrypt(plaintext)?
   b. If room_hpke_pub.is_some():
      - Build sealed plaintext: {pub_id_bytes(64) as string || mls_ciphertext}
        Format: len-prefixed or fixed: encode as pub_id_str.as_bytes() (64 ASCII bytes) || mls_ciphertext
      - sealed = nie_core::hpke::seal_message(&room_hpke_pub.unwrap(), &sealed_plaintext)?
      - Send SealedBroadcastParams { sealed }
   c. Else: fall back to regular BroadcastParams { payload: mls_ciphertext } (transition compat)
5. After MLS group established, derive room key:
   - (room_hpke_pub, room_hpke_secret) = mls_client.room_hpke_keypair()?
   - room_hpke_pub = Some(pub_bytes)
   - Publish room HPKE pub key: send PUBLISH_HPKE_KEY { public_key: room_hpke_pub.to_vec() }
     (overwrites identity-level key; room key is what peers use for broadcast sealing)
6. In the receive path, handle SealedDeliver notifications:
   a. Parse SealedDeliverParams { sealed }
   b. Unseal: let plaintext = nie_core::hpke::unseal_message(&hpke_secret, &sealed)?
      (use room_hpke_secret if MLS active, else identity hpke_secret)
   c. Parse sealed plaintext: first 64 bytes = sender_pub_id (ASCII hex), rest = mls_ciphertext
   d. Process MLS: let decrypted = mls_client.process_incoming(&mls_ciphertext)?
   e. Verify sender: check that sender_pub_id matches the MLS credential from process_incoming
   f. If mismatch: warn!("sealed sender mismatch, dropping"); continue
   g. Display as normal chat message
7. Handle sealed DM (SealedWhisperDeliver):
   a. Parse SealedWhisperDeliverParams { to, sealed }
   b. Unseal with identity hpke_secret (DMs sealed to user's identity key, not room key)
   c. Extract sender_pub_id + payload; process as regular whisper
8. For sending sealed DM:
   a. Before sending whisper: GET_HPKE_KEY for recipient pub_id
   b. If they have HPKE key: seal and send SealedWhisperParams
   c. Else: fall back to regular WhisperParams

Defensive programming rules:
- hpke_secret must never be logged (use tracing::debug!("unsealing sealed message") not debug!("{:?}", hpke_secret))
- Sender verification (step 6f) is mandatory — do not skip even when MLS credential lookup fails
- process_incoming returning None means non-application message (commit etc.) — handle gracefully
- Unseal errors are warn!() + continue, not crash (bad message from relay, or relay tampered)
- Fallback to unsealed Broadcast must be explicit comment: // fallback: MLS not yet active
- room_hpke_secret update must happen AFTER MLS group established (not before)
- 64-byte sender pub_id prefix: sender_pub_id.as_bytes() is ASCII (64 chars) — parse back with String::from_utf8(&plaintext[0..64])
acceptance_criteria: cargo check --workspace exits 0; cargo test --workspace passes; chat loop publishes HPKE key on connect; sealed broadcast sent when MLS active; SealedDeliver received and unsealed with sender verification
close_reason: Sealed sender wired into CLI chat loop: HPKE key published on connect, room HPKE key derived after MLS activation, sealed broadcast send path, SEALED_DELIVER and SEALED_WHISPER_DELIVER receive handlers. cargo check/clippy/test all clean.
